codeReview.summary: The fix commit adequately remediates the prior blocking shell-injection findings by replacing shell-string execSync usage with argv-based execFileSync calls in both preflight auth and deploy execution paths, while preserving deploy behavior via targeted and existing deploy tests.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The worker reported skill adherence as fully followed, but the skill procedure requires manual CLI/API sanity checks for user-facing changes (or explicit rationale when skipped). This deploy fix changed user-facing CLI behavior but the handoff only includes automated test commands and no manual-check rationale.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: `.factory/skills/mors-global-worker/SKILL.md` step 8 requires manual checks for user-facing surfaces; handoff `...__developer-launch-fix-deploy-shell-injection-safety__36e8364a-67f5-4a9d-aa42-3a7cc942e978.json` contains only `verification.commandsRun` entries and no interactive/manual section; transcript skeleton likewise shows only automated test/validator commands.

summary: PASS. Reviewed transcript skeleton, prior failed review, and both diffs (01f2d94 and fd2d44b). The original shell-injection vectors in `src/deploy.ts` and `src/cli.ts` were converted to argv-based execution, so env-derived flyctl/app/region values are no longer interpreted by a shell. The fix resolves the prior blocking failure.
